title: Am I the only one who listens to Fly me to the Moon
display_title: Am I the only one who listens to Fly me to the Moon
wikitext:
[]: Am I the only one who listens to Fly me to the Moon?             Quote Post by narcosis219 » Sat Sep 30, 2006 8:37 pm I mean, all the topics and forums i've read about favourite eva songs dont mention fly me to the moon. I feel lonely because i think im the only person who loves listens to it everyday. it's my favourite song of all time. no love for fly me to the moon?

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Sat Sep 30, 2006 9:11 pm I love Fly Me to the Moon, its just not a song that I can really relate with eva. now my fav song, Armageddon, that is totally eva!

[]:             Quote Post by Millions Livio » Sat Sep 30, 2006 9:59 pm I love Fly Me to The Moon, however not my favorite song. I personally like The Passage of Emptiness and Ode to Joy as my favorites. The problem with Fly Me to The Moon is they overused it. There's ten to fifteen songs of nothing but almost exact same variants of the song. I like the Frank Sinatra version, the instrumental version, and the These Women Long version, however the others are pretty much the same. This is why I think it lacked a lot of support. Evangelionfreak wrote: I love Fly Me to the Moon, its just not a song that I can really relate with eva. now my fav song, Armageddon, that is totally eva!

[]:             Quote Post by Darius » Sat Sep 30, 2006 10:08 pm I have yet to hear these other versions (as in the original and the others outside of Eva) but from what I have heard, Fly me to the moon isn't a song I like...But of course, that can change once I hear the original...It's the Frank Sinatra one I believe...correct?

[]:             Quote Post by narcosis219 » Sat Sep 30, 2006 10:27 pm Bart Howard

[]:             Quote Post by Millions Livio » Sat Sep 30, 2006 10:38 pm narcosis219 wrote: Bart Howard

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sat Sep 30, 2006 11:39 pm It's ridiculous how many versions of the song, in varying levels of broken English, have been released on the Eva soundtracks over the years. After a while, you can just get sick of it... I always thought it was a great way to end the show, especially when it became so incongrous with what was happening in the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Sun Oct 01, 2006 7:39 am I love that song too! The version where that woman is singing. The Ayanami version creeps me out a lot... brrr... You're not alone, man!

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Sun Oct 01, 2006 7:43 am It's a good song, but they made far too many versions of it. I just like the standard plain version. It's not the best song on the soundtrack(s) though but most certainly isn't the worst.

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Sun Oct 01, 2006 11:17 am Personally after an episode finishes and it gets to "Fly Me To The Moon", I mute the T.V. and turn up Labyrinth - Sons of Thunder. Can't beat Italian power metal.

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Sun Oct 01, 2006 12:43 pm Millions Livio wrote: Evangelionfreak wrote: I love Fly Me to the Moon, its just not a song that I can really relate with eva. now my fav song, Armageddon, that is totally eva!

[]:             Quote Post by Millions Livio » Sun Oct 01, 2006 1:04 pm Evangelionfreak wrote: Millions Livio wrote: Evangelionfreak wrote: I love Fly Me to the Moon, its just not a song that I can really relate with eva. now my fav song, Armageddon, that is totally eva!

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Sun Oct 01, 2006 1:08 pm Millions Livio wrote: Evangelionfreak wrote: Millions Livio wrote: Evangelionfreak wrote: I love Fly Me to the Moon, its just not a song that I can really relate with eva. now my fav song, Armageddon, that is totally eva!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Oct 01, 2006 1:27 pm Fly Me to the Moon is a fitting ending theme, but I never listen to it. I don't listen to Cruel Angel's Thesis much either.

[]:             Quote Post by Millions Livio » Sun Oct 01, 2006 1:49 pm Evangelionfreak wrote: Millions Livio wrote: Evangelionfreak wrote: Millions Livio wrote: Evangelionfreak wrote: I love Fly Me to the Moon, its just not a song that I can really relate with eva. now my fav song, Armageddon, that is totally eva!

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sun Oct 01, 2006 4:00 pm I think I liked "Fly Me To The Moon" more before I saw Eva, actually.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Oct 01, 2006 4:20 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: I think I liked "Fly Me To The Moon" more before I saw Eva, actually.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Mon Oct 02, 2006 3:45 pm I have never been a fan of the song "Fly Me to the Moon." I think I listened to it once the first time I saw Eva and that was it.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Oct 03, 2006 2:37 pm I like it, it's just I can't stand the Yoko Takahashi versions. Her singing really grates on my nerves with that song, she does so much better with her other ones! :roll: Clarie's version is probably one of my favorites, series-wise and in general. (It's up there with Frankie-baby's version, IMO.) Here's what I like about the character's versions: Asuka's version: the cutest! Though that "I love you!" at the end can get to ya after awhile... *twitch* Rei's versions: She seems to be the best, singing-wise. Misato's versions: Has the best pronuncation out of the three. IE: She says "worship" as opposed to "worsip", as Yuko and Megumi say. I'm not saying the way any of them sing it is bad or anything, especially considering the Japanese vowels and such are tottaly different from English, (Vowels in Japanese have the same pronuncation as in Spanish.) I'm just pointing that out. "Shinji"'s version (Ogata Megumi-san) isn't that bad, but...it's not that great either. Something about her singing is kinda weird in that. It kind of sounds as if Ogata-san has too much vibratto while singing, is the best way I can describe it. *shrug* One thing I'm wondering, though is because Zanakou na tenshi no teeze's lyrics has A LOT to the with the series and Shinji (*cough* 3I and overall), you think Fly Me~ was used for any symbolic purpose as well? ....It's so hard NOT to over-anaylze with this series. :roll:

[]:             Quote Post by Millions Livio » Tue Oct 03, 2006 4:44 pm Overall I tend to agree, and I always figured that Fly Me to the Moon was used for a symbolic purpose.

I feel lonely because i think im the only person who loves listens to it everyday. it's my favourite song of all time. no love for fly me to the moon?
